PropertyBoolean.name: enabled
PropertyBoolean.description: Enabled / Disable plugin in it's entirety

PropertyString.name: role
PropertyString.help: Indicates if the protocol stack runs a PPP client or PPP server
PropertyString.description: Functional Role for protocol stack, client or server

PropertyInt.name: numSessions
PropertyInt.help: Number of PPP sessions to establish
PropertyInt.description: No. of PPP sessions to establish

PropertyInt.name: setupRate
PropertyInt.help: Number of PPP sessions to setup, per second
PropertyInt.description: No. of PPP sessions to setup, per second

PropertyBoolean.name: enableThrottling
PropertyBoolean.help: If checked, the setup of PPP sessions is throttled once the number of sessions outstanding, reaches 'maxOutstandingSession'
PropertyBoolean.description: Throttle session creation when 'maxOutstandingSession' is reached

PropertyInt.name: maxOutstandingSessions
PropertyInt.help: The maximum number of PPP sessions that be in outstanding state at any given time.
PropertyInt.description: Max. number of PPP sessions allowed in outstanding state

PropertyInt.name: teardownRate
PropertyInt.help: Number of PPP sessions to teardown, per second
PropertyInt.description: Number of PPP sessions to teardown, per second

PropertyNode.name: atmRange
PropertyNode.description: ATM Range to use from underlying Hardware plugin

PropertyNode.name: macRange
PropertyNode.description: MAC Range to use from underlying Hardware plugin. [Auto generated] option will select the first MAC address in the list.

PropertyNode.help: See VlanIdRange object for more info.
PropertyNode.name: vlanRange
PropertyNode.description: VLAN Range to use from underlying Hardware plugin

PropertyNode.name: pvcRange
PropertyNode.description: PVC Range to use from underlying Hardware plugin

PropertyNode.name: pppoe
PropertyNode.description: Object that configure PPPoe specific properties

PropertyNode.name: ppp
PropertyNode.description: Object that configure PPP specific properties

Method2.name: GetTestMode
Method2.help: Return basic mode of test, e.g. pppoe, pppoa, etc.. (NOTE: for internal use only)

Argument.name: testMode
Argument.help: Basic mode of test, e.g. pppoe, pppoa, etc.. (NOTE: for internal use only)
